name: jane-street
tagline: Let the type system prove what it can; snapshot and property-test the rest; scale review depth to the risk of the file.
philosophy: Make illegal states unrepresentable. The compiler is the first checker; golden outputs never change without explicit promotion; counterexamples become permanent regressions.

gates.description: Compiler/type-checker warnings in changed files are failures, not noise.
A changed snapshot/golden/expect output is never auto-accepted: the diff of old vs new output plus a written justification must appear in the report, or it blocks GREEN.
Algorithmic, parsing, or serialization code should carry property tests; shrunk counterexamples get pinned as named regression cases.
Paths listed in .parrot/scrutiny.json (if present) are high-risk: touching them triggers an extra checker pass and deeper review.
